message:
br-bd-9mdyg implement FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE in ext4 + ffs-extent

Mirror of bd-ezmts (collapse_range): shifts every extent past offset
right by length and grows the file by length, leaving an unallocated
sparse hole behind. No physical blocks allocated — reads in the new
hole return zeroes until written.

ffs_extent::insert_range:
- Phase 1 splits any extent that straddles the cut into a left piece
  ([ext_start, cut)) and a right piece ([cut, ext_end)) that share the
  unwritten flag and the same physical extent (right piece's physical
  is offset by left_len). punch_hole only handles a non-zero hole, so
  the split is done explicitly.
- Phase 2 snapshots every extent at or past the cut, sorts the
  snapshot in descending logical_block order so an in-place shift
  cannot collide with a not-yet-moved later extent, then
  delete-then-reinsert each at logical_block + count. checked_add
  rejects an overflow into u32::MAX.

OpenFs::ext4_fallocate now recognises FALLOC_FL_INSERT_RANGE = 0x20:
- KNOWN bitset extended; insert_range bool branch added.
- Strict checks per fs/ext4/extents.c::ext4_insert_range: must be the
  only mode bit (no KEEP_SIZE), offset + length must be block-aligned,
  offset must lie strictly before EOF (insert at EOF is rejected — use
  truncate up).
- Extent cache invalidated wholesale (logical positions move past the
  cut). inode.size grows by length; inode.blocks unchanged.

Tests: 663 ffs-core --lib pass (was 659 + 4 new). New regressions:
- insert_range_shifts_tail_and_grows_file: 3 chunks A B C, insert
  4 KiB hole at offset == block, verify size grows by 4 KiB, A is
  unchanged, hole reads as zeros, B shifted into hole position, C
  shifted behind B.
- insert_range_unaligned_returns_einval: misaligned offset + length
  both surface EINVAL.
- insert_range_at_or_past_eof_returns_einval: covers the offset == EOF
  and offset > EOF rejection paths.
- insert_range_with_other_flags_returns_einval: INSERT_RANGE +
  KEEP_SIZE rejected.
- write_fallocate_unsupported_mode_bits_rejected updated to use
  FALLOC_FL_UNSHARE_RANGE (0x40) which is still unsupported.

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_9mdyg.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
